reflection.summary: Wrote 'hello' to dummy.txt in the current working directory.
lessons: Append trailing newline by default when writing text files — most text files expect it. Ask only if user explicitly opts out.
Do not announce 'I'll write the file now' before tool call — just call the tool. Saves a turn.
recommendations: Add to system prompt: 'When writing text files, always append a trailing newline unless the user explicitly requests otherwise.'
Add to system prompt: 'Do not narrate tool calls before executing them. Call the tool directly.'
